content: Onboarding is a system, not a tour. Checklists that build real artifacts (Notion-style) outperform forced tours; 3-step tours hit ~72% completion vs ~16% for 7-step. Tours triggered on first login before the user has chosen a path are interruptions. Guided empty states are the most underutilized onboarding surface: a blank dashboard with no guidance is a silent drop-off point; turn every empty state into a next-action prompt or seed demo data. Best flows combine 2-4 lightweight patterns; time-to-first-value is the metric that predicts retention. Middle path (contextual guidance on demand) beats over-engineered first sessions and invisible onboarding.
